payload.text: badge 'exerice X' plus chic et aere un peux en haut et restyle les nu;ero et sous numero des qst

**Revising Style Elements**

I'm currently focused on the aesthetic improvements the user desires. I'm investigating how to update the styling of the "Exercise X" badge and the exercise question numbering. The goal is to make these elements more visually appealing and user-friendly within the application.


**Refining Visual Design**

I'm now zeroing in on the suggested aesthetic changes. The "Exercise X" badge is receiving attention; I'll restyle it to be more "chic." I'll add spacing at the top of the title or badge section for improved readability. Finally, I'll update the number and sub-number styling in the questions to enhance visual clarity and user experience. My focus is now on `components/ExerciseCard.tsx`, where the primary components live.


**Adjusting Number Spacing**

I'm currently working to adjust the spacing around the question numbers. I've been experimenting with the `lineHeight` property to ensure that the numbers are properly centered within their badges, regardless of the level. I'm focusing on ensuring consistent visual clarity.


**Evaluating Badge Refinement**

I'm now diving into the proposed changes for the exercise badge, seeking a more elegant aesthetic. I'm exploring an outlined style, a softer background with a serif font, or a minimal look to replace the heavier dark block. I'm also considering modifications for the numbering styles, focusing on improving visual harmony across different levels. My aim is to make the "Exercise X" badge feel refined.


**Developing Badge Concepts**

I'm now iterating on the "Exercise X" badge. I've considered several options: separating "Exercise" and the number, experimenting with borders and shadows, and moving away from the existing dark slate background. I've explored a serif font (Playfair Display), and I'm currently leaning towards a black background with white, serif font text and some padding. I will add some top spacing to the text.


**Refining Numbering Style**

I'm now zeroing in on the best presentation for the question numbers. I've decided to move away from the light blue square and am now focusing on the main questions. I'm exploring a black circle with white text for main questions, a change from the darker slate circle. The font for these will be serif to fit the style of the badge, and I'm currently looking at Playfair Display or Roboto Slab. I intend to use this for the primary exercise questions.


**Updating Question Numbering**

I'm now revising the presentation of question numbers for improved visual appeal and clarity. I'm focusing on two primary levels. For main questions (1, 2, etc.), I intend to use a black circle with white text and a serif font. For sub-questions (a, b, etc.), I'm aiming for a cleaner look with no background, using a serif italic font in the format `a.`.


**Implementing Spacing Adjustments**

I'm now implementing the adjustments for more spacing, as requested. I've focused on the title section of the `ExerciseCard`. I'll adjust the padding, and specifically, add a `margin-top` to the title container for more visual breathing room. I'll maintain the current flex row arrangement.


**Defining Visual Structure**

I'm now zeroing in on defining the visual structure for questions. I'll maintain an inline structure for the badge and the exercise title, rather than separation. The badge's font will be a black background with white text, using 'Playfair Display' serif. The exercise title will adopt the same font family. I'm focusing on ensuring adequate spacing at the top of the title section, adjusting the padding and margin to enhance readability.


**Optimizing Numbering & Print**

I'm working to refine question numbering and print styles. For main questions, the black circle with white serif text is set. Sub-questions now have a transparent background with black serif italic text and a ")" suffix. I'll focus on ensuring print styles, specifically `styles/print.css`, remain consistent with the new visual changes. The goal is to keep code structure in `ExerciseCard.tsx` similar and update print styles as needed.


**Implementing Badge Revisions**

I'm now in the process of updating the `ExerciseCard` component. I'm focusing on implementing the badge and numbering refinements within `components/ExerciseCard.tsx`. I will be updating the `getNumbering` function to produce the correct text formatting for the question numbers. I'll focus on applying styles for level 0 as black circles with white text and level 1 as transparent text using a serif font. The `padding-top` will be considered for the text. Finally, I will ensure code maintainability.


**Adjusting Visual Breathing**

I'm now focusing on increasing the visual breathing room in the `ExerciseCard`. I'll be increasing the `gap` in the title section and updating the badge style. The user's request for "aéré un peu en haut" suggests adding padding to the card header itself, so I'll incorporate a `pb-4` or `gap-6` to the header. I'll also update the screen version with inline styles. I am considering the use of italic style, with `justifyContent: 'flex-start'` and `paddingLeft: '0.2em'` for the questions. I will ensure that the printing is adjusted through the `styles/print.css`.


**Defining CSS Updates**

I'm now drafting the specific CSS updates. I've updated the `getNumbering` function to properly format question numbers and handle the sub-question "." correctly. I'm focusing on the `ExerciseCard` render, updating the badge style to a black background with white text, a serif font, and rounded corners. I'll increase the gap in the title layout. I'll need to update `styles/print.css` to reflect these changes, ensuring consistency in print output. I'll be adjusting level 0 and level 1 badge presentation based on the earlier adjustments.


**Updating Print Styles**

I'm now aligning the print styles with the latest visual design. I am carefully updating `styles/print.css` to ensure the printed output reflects the new style of the exercise badge and question numbers. This includes the black circle for main questions with white text and a serif font, and a transparent background with black serif italic text for the sub-questions. I'll also add a `pt-2` to the exercise title container within the card. I will test these changes thoroughly.


**Finalizing Print & Card Updates**

I'm wrapping up the implementation in `ExerciseCard.tsx`. I've updated the `getNumbering` function for proper sub-question formatting, and I've ensured print styles in `styles/print.css` align perfectly with the updated badge and numbering. I've adjusted the padding in the header. The black badges with white serif text and transparent sub-question backgrounds are set. The component should now present a polished final product.


**Refining Spacing Configuration**

I'm now focusing on a minor spacing refinement. I've updated the styles, and applied them so the numbers align visually; the adjustment I made for the black badge is `-0.1em`, and for the italicized transparent sub-question it's `0`. I am still working to ensure the alignment and that spacing is consistent with the latest changes to the `ExerciseCard`.


**Synchronizing Print & Screen Styles**

I'm making sure that printed versions also adopt the "chic" look. I've updated the `styles/print.css` to align the badge and numbering styles with the updated screen styles. I'm focusing on ensuring consistent serif fonts and background colors for both main and sub-questions. I'll test to confirm these adjustments and maintain the requested "chic" output. I'm also ensuring the numbering formats are consistent.

Here are the changes to restyle the "Exercise" badge for a chic, airy look (using the Playfair Display serif font, black background, and more spacing) and to update the question numbering (black circles for main questions, italic serif text for sub-questions).

### 1. `components/ExerciseCard.tsx`
Updated the badge styling and question numbering logic/styles.

entries.description: Update exercise badge to be chic (black serif) and restyle question numbers (circles/italic).
Update print styles for the new chic design.
